2603.17641.title: Automated Grammar-based Algebraic Multigrid Design With Evolutionary Algorithms
2603.17641.paper_summary: Although multigrid is asymptotically optimal for solving many important partial differential equations, its efficiency relies heavily on the careful selection of the individual algorithmic components. In contrast to recent approaches that can optimize certain multigrid components using deep learning techniques, we adopt a complementary strategy, employing evolutionary algorithms to construct efficient multigrid cycles from proven algorithmic building blocks. Here, we will present its application to generate efficient algebraic multigrid methods with so-called \emph{flexible cycling}, that is, level-specific smoothing sequences and non-recursive cycling patterns. The search space with such non-standard cycles is intractable to navigate manually, and is generated using genetic programming (GP) guided by context-free grammars. Numerical experiments with the linear algebra library, \emph{hypre}, demonstrate the potential of these non-standard GP cycles to improve multigrid performance both as a solver and a preconditioner.
2603.17641.paper_summary_zh: 雖然多重網格在解決許多重要的偏微分方程時是漸近最優的，但其效率在很大程度上依賴於個別算法組件的精心選擇。與最近可以使用深度學習技術優化某些多重網格組件的方法相比，我們採用了一種互補策略，利用進化算法從經過驗證的算法構建塊中構建高效的多重網格循環。在這裡，我們將介紹其應用，以生成所謂的\emph{靈活循環}的高效代數多重網格方法，即特定層次的平滑序列和非遞歸循環模式。具有這種非標準循環的搜索空間是難以手動導航的，並且是使用基於上下文無關文法的遺傳編程（GP）生成的。與線性代數庫\emph{hypre}的數值實驗展示了這些非標準GP循環在作為求解器和預處理器時改善多重網格性能的潛力。
2603.17641.author: Dinesh Parthasarathy et.al.
2603.17641.authors: Dinesh Parthasarathy, Wayne Mitchell, Arjun Gambhir, Harald Köstler, Ulrich Rüde
